findings.title: Trainer programmatic CLI and routing-metric correctness
evidence: Programmatic main previously depended on a script-local re import.
Routing entropy previously described selected top-k conditional entropy rather than the full 256-expert distribution.
Routing hooks previously remained enabled during gradient-checkpoint backward recomputation.
The repaired trainer imports re at module scope, records full 256-expert entropy separately from selected-top-k entropy, and pauses routing capture during checkpoint recomputation.
findings.ownership: Root repaired the concurrently owned trainer; pass 1 did not edit those files.

findings.title: Post-attach LoRA scope audit omitted runtime behavior
findings.before: Exact targets, rank, alpha, and trainable IDs could pass while dropout/scaling/adapter enablement or merge state was mutated.
findings.after: Every wrapper must use zero/Identity dropout, bias false, RSLoRA false, DoRA false, exact classic alpha/rank scaling, requested adapter active and enabled, and unmerged state.

validation.expected_intermediate_failure: RuntimeError: attachment contract changed before regeneration

findings.title: Aggregate routing-metrics run artifact
findings.before: The trainer streamed routing_metrics.jsonl without the plan-required content-addressed run summary/receipt.
findings.after: The trainer emits a self-addressed routing_metrics.json, binds its file/content hashes into run_result, and proves the JSONL hash, exact interval step/cursor coverage, exact 40-layer inventory, top-8 assignment accounting, finite entropy ranges, and deterministic token-weighted aggregates.
findings.validation: Covered by the 11-test synthetic trainer suite, including disabled routing, multiple intervals, malformed layer inventory, and wrong step coverage.

findings.title: Valid PEFT adapter-only resume was rejected
findings.before: The trainer rejected normal frozen-base missing_keys returned while restoring an adapter-only PEFT checkpoint, so every valid resume failed before optimizer, scheduler, and RNG restoration.
findings.after: The restore permits normal frozen-base omissions, rejects unexpected or missing LoRA keys, requires the normalized adapter key inventory to match exactly, and compares every restored tensor's shape, dtype, and value.
findings.validation: Covered by the 11-test synthetic trainer suite with both a valid exact round trip and an incomplete-adapter rejection.

findings.title: Fast-linear selector does not fail closed on all sealed-evidence mutations
findings.evidence: The canonical selector can retain hybrid_training after GPU architecture-baseline drift, a successful worker probe is bound to the wrong physical GPU, or benchmark timings/ratios have invalid negative semantics.
findings.required_fix: Validate the exact four-GPU outer/inner identity binding and finite nonnegative benchmark inputs before applying material-improvement logic; reseal the content-addressed contract with adversarial synthetic tests.

findings.title: Out-of-vocabulary IDs were accepted until model execution
findings.before: The immutable mixed-snapshot reader enforced nonnegative token IDs but not the Qwen3.6 vocabulary upper bound.
findings.after: Every input ID must be in [0, 248320), so malformed rows fail before model loading; exact label rules remain unchanged.
findings.validation: A focused synthetic adversarial row covers the upper-bound failure.

findings.title: Architecture audit accepts malformed safetensor header layouts
findings.before: The checkpoint audit lacked fail-closed duplicate-key/non-finite JSON parsing and exact dtype-width, shape-derived span, file-bound, contiguous nonoverlap, and index total-size validation.
findings.after: The architecture audit now enforces all of those invariants, rejects gaps/overlap/trailing bytes, writes atomically, and retains the exact sealed architecture contract.
findings.validation: Five adversarial synthetic header layouts and the real contract --check pass without reading tensor weight payloads.

findings.title: Memory-smoke prerequisite receipts and hybrid binding are not fully fail closed
findings.before: Pretrainer receipt validation accepted arbitrary well-formed adapter identities and inconsistent peak/current counters, while the smoke path lacked complete binding to the current sealed hybrid contract.
findings.after: Receipts now bind exact GPU and adapter identities, enforce peak/current and throughput/time invariants and duplicate-key rejection, default to 2,048 tokens, and revalidate current sealed package/source/hardware/probe/benchmark/runtime prerequisites before hybrid mutation.
findings.validation: Architecture, attachment, memory, and fast contract checks pass; the later-pass focused combined suite reports 33 passed.

findings.title: Training authorities need a complete Qwen3.6 tokenizability audit
findings.evidence: The current runtime upper-bound fix prevents out-of-vocabulary execution, but the complete eligible training pool still needs strict UTF-8/text, encoding, ID-bound, lossless round-trip, reserved-control-token, official chat-template/masking, and unsplittable 2,048-token validation.
findings.required_fix: Use an explicitly scoped nonprotected authority inventory, manually repair each finding with lineage receipts, rebuild affected authorities, and seal a deterministic zero-failure --check.

findings.title: 357 seed-QA rows lack semantic correctness authority
findings.evidence: All 357 previously admitted seed rows, totaling 9,153 assistant tokens, explicitly state that semantic verification is required; source lineage, rights, hashes, and generated-domain receipts do not establish answer correctness. The assembler/runtime now fail closed and admit zero unresolved seed rows.
findings.required_fix: Seal per-row correctness decisions, repair or exclude bad/unresolved records, add verified source-disjoint replacements as needed, and bind the authority so the final domain-QA budget is exactly 750,000 tokens with zero unresolved seeds.

findings.title: Plan section 19 dataset and run-artifact contract is incomplete
findings.evidence: Typed segments preserve substantial metadata, but variant/top manifests and run results do not yet expose a single fail-closed contract covering every prescribed segment field and required content-addressed run artifact.
findings.required_fix: Seal exact per-segment metadata derivations and required run-artifact schemas/receipts; missing, duplicate, ambiguous, forged, or stale entries must block launch.

findings.evidence: The historical provisional manifest reported a 740,847-token generated shortfall; after fail-closing all 357 unresolved seed rows, the domain-QA authority must cover the full exact 750,000 assistant-token budget. No training sequence is launch-authorized.
A fresh opaque source-disjoint extension covering the final selected bytes is required before the mixed snapshot can authorize launch.
The preregistration is sealed, but the base-model baseline artifact remains response_generation_pending.
Pretrainer one-step memory receipts pass, but exact final mixed-trainer multi-step stability, fragmentation, checkpoint/resume, and vision-exclusion proof remain pending.
